data.title: Silver Spruce Resources approves option grants
data.description:
• Silver Spruce (SSE:CA) announced on Friday that its board has authorized the issuance of an aggregate 2.1M stock options.
  • Each option is exercisable into one common share at $0.30 per share for five years from the grant date.
  • Shares -5.88%.

data.title: Conduent appoints Harsha V. Agadi as CEO
data.description:
• Conduent Incorporated (CNDT) announced on Friday that the Board of Directors has appointed Harsha V. Agadi, Chairman of Conduent’s Board of Directors, as Chief Executive Officer, effective immediately.
  • As part of this transition, Agadi will step down as Chairman of the Board, and Margarita Paláu-Hernández has been appointed independent Chair of the Board. 
  • The company said Agadi succeeds Cliff Skelton, who will step down as President, Chief Executive Officer, and a member of the Board of Directors. All leadership changes are effective immediately.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nord Precious Metals appoints Heidi Gutte as CFO
data.description:
• Nord Precious Metals (NTH:CA) announced on Friday that Heidi Gutte has been appointed CFO effective Jan. 15, 2026, succeeding Robert Suttie.
  • Gutte has more than 15 years of experience with publicly traded mineral exploration and mining companies, with expertise in corporate finance, IFRS reporting, audit readiness, tax optimization, and corporate compliance.

data.title: SITE Centers sells partnership interest for about $20.8 mln
data.description:
• SITE Centers (SITC) on Friday said it sold its partnership interests in the RVIP IIIB joint venture that owns Deer Park Town Center in Deer Park, Illinois, to its existing joint venture partner for approximately $20.8 million, prior to closing costs.
  • SITC -0.78% after hours to $6.29.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Under Armour tops the list of most shorted S&P 500 consumer discretionary stocks in January; Amazon among least shorted
data.description:
Average short interest across the S&P 500 consumer discretionary stocks rose in January, with Under Armour (UAA) emerging as the most shorted stock in the sector, while Amazon.com (AMZN) remained among the least shorted names, based on short interest as a percentage of shares outstanding.

Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLY) has been up 2.10% so far this year, as compared to the over 1.45% rise in the broader S&P 500 index over the same period.

Stocks with the largest and least short positions

Under Armour (UAA) topped the list with 34.96% of shares outstanding sold short, followed by Kohl’s Corporation (KSS) at 25.86% and Advance Auto Parts (AAP) at 21.75%.

RH (RH) ranked next with a short interest of 20.50%, while Etsy, Inc. (ETSY) stood at 17.43%.

On the least-shorted end, Polibeli Group Ltd. (PLBL) recorded short interest of 0.01%, followed by VinFast Auto Ltd. (VFS) at 0.10% and Luckin Coffee Inc. (LKNCY) at 0.30%.

Amazon.com (AMZN) remained among the least shorted consumer discretionary stocks with 0.69% of shares outstanding sold short, while Lennar Corporation (LEN.B) stood at 0.93%.

Overall, short positioning across the consumer discretionary sector in January remained concentrated in select apparel, retail, and auto-related names, while several of the sector’s largest companies continued to carry relatively low short interest.

data.title: SEC nod lets Nasdaq add Monday, Wednesday stock options expiries - report
data.description:
Nasdaq (NDAQ) won regulatory approval to add Monday and Wednesday options expirations in certain stocks, according to a media report on Friday.

The exchange operator was approved by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission to list the contracts for Mag-7 shares Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL), Amazon (AMZN), Apple (AAPL), Meta Platforms (META), Microsoft (MSFT) Nvidia (NVDA) and Tesla (TSLA), in addition to Broadcom (AVGO) and the State Street Financial Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLF), Bloomberg reported, citing a letter from the SEC.

A Nasdaq spokesperson told Bloomberg that the expiries will be for the next month ahead, with Jan. 26 being the first. Currently, single stock options mature on Fridays, though this SEC approval brings U.S. markets closer to daily single-stock options trading. Only the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY), Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) and iShares Russell 2000 ETF (IWM) all have daily expiries. 

“This decision supports greater flexibility and precision for market participants, enabling more efficient hedging and trading strategies,” said David Barrett, head of product for US Options at Nasdaq, as quoted by Bloomberg. “It’s a step forward for the industry as it continues to modernize and respond to evolving investor needs.”

Neither Nasdaq (NDAQ) nor the SEC immediately responded to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

data.title: Innovation Beverage receives Nasdaq noncompliance notice
data.description:
• Innovation Beverage (NASDAQ: IBG) on Friday  stated that it received a Nasdaq notice on Jan. 14, 2026, after failing to hold an annual shareholders’ meeting within 12 months of its Dec. 31, 2024 fiscal year-end, resulting in noncompliance with Listing Rules 5620(a), 5810(c)(2)(G), and IM-5620.
  • IBG intends to submit a timely compliance plan and to hold an annual meeting of shareholders by March 31, 2026.

data.title: Cellyan Biotechnology receives Nasdaq bid price deficiency notice
data.description:
• Cellyan Biotechnology (HKPD) received a Nasdaq notification on January 12, 2026, for non-compliance with the $1 minimum bid price requirement.
  • The deficiency arose after the company’s shares traded below $1 for 30 consecutive business days from November 26, 2025 to January 9, 2026.
  • Cellyan has been granted a 180-day compliance period, until July 13, 2026, to regain compliance.
  • The company may pursue measures such as a reverse share split if needed and could qualify for an additional 180-day grace period.

data.title: Shell, Mitsubishi explore potential sales of stakes in LNG Canada - Reuters
data.description:
Shell (SHEL) and Mitsubishi (MSBHF) are exploring sale options for their respective stakes in the C$40B LNG Canada project, Reuters reported Friday.

Shell (SHEL), LNG Canada's largest owner with a 40% stake, reportedly has been working with investment bankers at Rothschild to sound out interested parties in recent weeks, and the company could offload as much as three-quarters of its holding, or 30% of the project.

However, Shell (SHEL) is said to have expressed willingness to consider different options related to its exposure to the project's Phase 1, which is operational, and the proposed Phase 2, given the different risks.

Any buyer for Shell's (SHEL) stake could be committing ~$15B, including the equity stake, debt and capital requirements for Phase 2, according to the report.

Mitsubishi (MSBHF), which holds a 15% stake in LNG Canada, reportedly has hired RBC Capital as it weighs its options, although it is not clear much of its stake the company could market.

LNG Canada started production in June, but has since run into operational problems, and its Train 2 processing unit was down in December.

The project enjoys a supply cost advantage because prices for Canadian natural gas consistently trade at a discount to the U.S. Henry Hub benchmark, but the industry is concerned about a potential global oversupply as new LNG production comes online; Energy Transfer, for example, said last month that it was suspending development of its Lake Charles LNG export facility in Louisiana.

data.title: Lockheed Martin widens gains after ending seventh straight session in green
data.description:
Lockheed Martin (LMT) extended its rally for the seventh straight session on Friday. It closed 0.79% higher at $582.43.

The stock has been rallying since January 8, when it ended 4.34% higher, following U.S. President Donald Trump’s  push for sharply higher military spending. In a Truth Social post, Trump proposed a historic $1.5T defense budget for fiscal year 2027.

On January 9, LMT rose 4.2% to a 52-week high of $543.08 after Truist Securities upgraded the stock to Buy from Hold with a $605 price target, saying Lockheed's FY 2025 underperformance, coupled with attractive valuation, creates a compelling risk/reward profile in 2026.

As per Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating, LMT has a Hold rating with a score of 3.23 out of 5. The company has been rated A+ for profitability, but it scored a D+ for valuation and growth. Seeking Alpha, as well as Wall Street analysts, echoed a similar sentiment and issued a Hold call for the stock.

Seeking Alpha analyst Dhierin Bechai said in a recent article that LMT's appeal is fading, with its upside now limited to 9%. Despite his concerns, he had a Buy rating for the stock.

Bechai said LMT’s growth outlook increasingly depends on securing major new defense platforms, such as the Golden Dome project, as free cash flow growth has stalled. He added that share repurchases no longer provide meaningful upside, with essentially all free cash flow already returned to shareholders, while the dividend yield remains unremarkable.

“In terms of upside, we are seeing that Lockheed Martin has roughly the same upside as Northrop Grumman. I have been bullish on the aerospace and defense industry, but we have detected more value in the supply chain, European defense peers, other emerging defense players, and mixed end-market contractors than with the US prime defense contractors,” the analyst said.
